mnemonics.title: Hamiltonian Path Definition
mnemonics.content: VERTEX - Visit Each Vertex Exactly Once Returning To Start = Hamiltonian CIRCUIT; Without returning = Hamiltonian PATH

mnemonics.title: Dirac vs Ore
mnemonics.content: DIRAC: Each vertex needs DEGREES ≥ n/2 (Individual); ORE: Sum of degrees of non-adjacent pairs ≥ n (Pairs)

mnemonics.title: NP-Complete Reminder
mnemonics.content: HAMILTONIAN = Hard! No Polynomial solution known - Exhaustive search required

acronyms.usage: Optimization problem finding shortest Hamiltonian cycle in weighted graph
Class of problems where solutions can be verified in polynomial time

visualTips.visualization: Imagine walking around a polygon visiting each vertex once and returning - like traversing all corners of a pentagon exactly once
Think of each vertex needing 'enough friends' (degree) to ensure connectivity for a cycle covering all vertices
Tree structure where each branch represents choosing next vertex; prune branches that lead to dead ends

analogies.analogy: Like a delivery truck visiting every house exactly once before returning to depot - visiting each location without repetition
Eulerian is like a mail carrier walking every street once; Hamiltonian is like a tourist visiting every city exactly once
Finding Hamiltonian path is like trying all possible combinations of visiting cities - exponentially many possibilities unlike easy Eulerian check
